feedback.text: I would like to offer feedback on the EU Digital Fairness Act and respectfully request that the legislation include provisions that address the growing concern over the loss of access to legally purchased digital products, particularly video games and other software. I am concerned about a pattern where digital products, especially games, are being delisted, rendered non-functional, or otherwise made inaccessible to consumers, often without adequate notice, compensation, or alternative access. These practices unfairly disadvantage consumers who paid for permanent access but are now finding themselves unable to use what they bought. To promote genuine digital fairness, I urge EU legislators to: - Require ongoing access to purchased digital goods or a reasonable alternative when online services are discontinued. - Mandate clear and early notice to consumers before a digital product is delisted or rendered inoperable - Ensure transparency in terms of service about the lifespan of digital products and users' rights post-delisting. - Establish the right to redress or refund when access is revoked without fault of the consumer Consumers should not bear the cost of corporate decisions to discontinue support or hosting of digital products. Just as physical goods are protected under consumer law, digital purchases deserve equal fairness, transparency, and longevity. Thank you for considering this important aspect Sincerely, Evangelos Ferlantis
